entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat related question
entry.content:
Hi, I am new to the SAP products and server support in general. I inherited a server using SAP Cloud Connector. While looking around on the server I realised that there is a tomcat instance running on the server. I have never installed tomcat on this server. Which brings me this question, is there an embedded tomcat in the package install of SAP Cloud Connector? When I asked the past group that was supporting this server and the people who asked for this to be installed on the server, I get 2 different answers.

1. Yes it is embedded in the package install for SAP CC!

2. No, it was installed separately by the people that used to manage the server or someone else.

So, I wanted to ask, which one is true? If tomcat is embedded in the package install of SAP CC, would anyone know the version of tomcat embedded in the most recent version of SAP CC?

entry.title: Automatic Payment Run – Credit Note Not Considered with Invoice
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Automatic Payment Run and would appreciate your guidance.

Error- No pymt possible because items with a debit bal.still exist; see job log - how to resolve this error in public could

Scenario

We have:

  • One supplier invoice: USD 146,172.49
  • One supplier credit note: USD 117,521.30

Expected behavior:

  • The payment run should net both documents and generate payment only for the balance amount (USD 28,651.19).

However, during the payment proposal/payment run:

  • The credit note is not being considered together with the invoice.
  • Initially, the invoice was payment blocked to avoid paying the full invoice amount without considering the credit note.
  • Even after removing the payment block, the issue remains the same.

Error / Logs Observed

From the payment log we can see:

  • “No payment possible because items with a debit balance still exist”
  • “None of the payment methods defined can be used for these items”
  • “Payment not possible because of reported error”

Additional Details

  • Both documents belong to the same supplier and same company code.
  • Currency is USD.
  • Payment method used is “T”.
  • Both items are open and due.
  • Net balance after offset should be payable.

Questions

  1. Why is the automatic payment run not offsetting the invoice and credit note automatically?
  2. How can we resolve this so that only the net payable amount is paid?

Any guidance would be highly appreciated.

Screenshot for your reference-

Thank you.

Rohini

entry.title: Re: Unfair Result in C_S4CFI_2602 – System-Based Exam Com...
entry.content: Hi Patilp_03, Thank you for sharing your experience — I can completely relate to your situation. While I understand that the system-based evaluation follows strict validation criteria, what concerns me the most is the complete lack of feedback. During my exam: - I completed 100% of the required exercises within the given time - All key processes were executed and visible in the system (postings, configurations, etc.) However, I received a failed result without any indication of: - Which specific step was incorrect - What configuration detail was missing or inconsistent - What exactly led to losing points This is the most frustrating part. Without knowing what went wrong, how are we expected to improve for the next attempt? From a learning perspective, this creates a major gap: We are asked to “practice more”, but we don’t know what needs correction, even after completing all tasks. I have escalated my case to SAP Support and requested further clarification, as this raises concerns about transparency and fairness in system-based certifications. Hopefully SAP can improve this process by providing at least minimal feedback to candidates. Let’s keep this discussion going — it seems this is not an isolated case. Best regards, Wellington

entry.title: Re: Handling different accrual recalculation scenarios on Leave of Absence in Employee Central
entry.content:
hi @laura_draht - I am not sure If I understood this fully.

If you would like to calculate only for 9 months where the user is active the average, one option could be to store 0 in the custom field which is used for the average. For example , Say 30 days is my accrual for year across 366 days. I would get 30 days as per the average rule. Now I go on parental leave for first 3 months. The custom field is 0. So this would yield me = 30 *0 / 366 + (number of days in 9 months Assume 270 ) 30* 270 / 366.  If you cannot use the eligibility, we can perhaps use 0 in custom field which is taken in average calculation which will reduce the average eventually

2) for considering full months, we also have a new rule function . Not sure if this helps but putting it here  https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/implementing-business-rules-in-sap-successfactors/get-completed-months-of-time-for-time-type?q=full+months

entry.title: SmartRecruiters Employee Portal VS RMK internal career site?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
 
I am currently working on an integration project between SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors for a client. We are trying to define the best approach for internal mobility. Today, we see that: - SmartRecruiters provides an Employee Portal - SAP RMK can also support internal career sites In this context, I would like to understand how others are handling this: Are you relying more on RMK for internal mobility, or still using the SmartRecruiters Employee Portal? Any feedback or experience would be very helpful. Thanks!

entry.title: SmartRecruiters / SuccessFactors – data migration approach and legacy data retention
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on several projects involving a transition from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to SmartRecruiters, and I am trying to get feedback from people who have already gone through or are about to go through this type of migration.

One of the key questions I have is about data migration strategy: in your experience, does it make sense to include the migration of open jobs and active candidates as part of the project scope? Or is it more common to avoid data migration altogether and start fresh in SmartRecruiters?

Another point I am trying to clarify concerns legacy data. For clients who decide not to migrate jobs and candidates from SuccessFactors Recruiting, what is usually done to ensure they still have access to historical data?Are clients typically exporting the data themselves before decommissioning, or is there any support from SAP to extract and archive this information?

This is quite important in some contexts, as clients may have legal obligations to retain recruitment data for several years depending on the country.

Any feedback or real-life experience would be very helpful; thanks !

entry.title: Cannot delete fields from Z* incompletion procedure (SD) – delete disabled / change not saved
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support,

We are configuring SD Incompletion Procedures and we face an issue with a custom (Z2) incompletion procedure.

Problem:
When we try to remove/delete specific fields from the Z2 incompletion procedure (copied/created as a customer procedure), the system does not allow the deletion. The delete option is either disabled (greyed out) or the entry reappears after saving / the system prevents saving without the field. As a result, we cannot adjust the incompletion log behavior for our business requirement.

Expected behavior:
We should be able to delete/remove fields from a customer-defined incompletion procedure (Z2 -Sales Item lvl) using the standard configuration activity." Define Incompleteness Procedures " in the CBC

Actual behavior:
Fields cannot be deleted from the Z2 procedure (delete is disabled).

Business impact:
Our sales/billing users are blocked by incomplete-document warnings/errors for fields that should not be mandatory in the new process, which prevents smooth document creation and testing.

Thank you.
Best regards,

entry.title: Example for Blog Refresher Session
entry.content:
• Introduction
  • Key Aspects
  • Manage Your Content
  • Have You Noticed?
  • Summary

Introduction

This blog aims to serve as an example for demonstrating how to create a blog post in the SAP Community platform.

Key Aspects

A meaningful title

A meaningful title enables audience to quickly grasp what the blog is about and whether it's relevant to them.

Apply the right structure

Every blog post is expected to consist of a beginning (introduction), a middle (main portion), and an end (a conclusion that wraps everything up). A collection of links or just a video with short text does not qualify as a blog post.

SAP Managed Tags

SAP Managed Tags are a type of tag derived from SAP's official list of products and solutions.

You can subscribe to an SAP managed tag so that you will receive notifications of new content about that product. 

You must add a tag to your content to indicate the product that is relevant for your blog.

All SAP Managed Tags | SAP Community

Blog Labels

Labels are used within the SAP Community to help categorize content in discussions, blogs, Q&As, and knowledge bases
Labels enable you to categorize the content you write based on the themes or content in the article. 

Unlike tags, labels are created by the Admin and typically controlled for consistency. Some Groups have a pre-defined list of labels, depending on the purpose and setup of the Group. In which case authors can choose/apply labels from the pre-defined list in the Group in which the article appears. Some Groups allow members to add their own labels. It is also possible to subscribe to a label to get notifications of new content associated with this label. 

Article User Tags

Article user tags are optional. They are used to further describe the topic or subject of the content, complementing the SAP Managed Tag. You can create your own user tags. For example, in a blog post about SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you might add these Article User Tags: implementation, guidance. Be sure to use commas between tags.

Cover Photos

Cover photos are optional. They are used only when a group or board owner decides to feature a blog post. But if you want to include a cover photo, the required dimensions are 600*420 pixels, as that size is the requirement for featured posts.

Scheduling for Publication

Select the date and time at the correct time zone to schedule your post for publication. This is helpful when you want to publish the content on a specific date when you're out of office.

Published link

You may have noticed that the format of a draft link is different from that of a published link. If you want to share the link to your colleague before you publish it, you should copy the published link.

Co-authors

The co-author functionality allows for other community members to assist during the editing and publishing process. Co-authors must be a member of the Community. You add a co-author by searching their user names.  

Tip: Consider adding a co-author if you have plans to go out of office, as this might impact the editing or publishing of the blog. It can also be helpful if you anticipate changes to your role or position (as has happened in practice). 

Note: The co-authors do not show in the user interface of the published post.
If you want to mention co-authors please do so as part of the post content, for instance thanking them at the end of the post.

Send Content for Review/Moderation

Posts from new bloggers will need to go through moderation before they are published. To bypass moderation, members will need to move up the rank ladder (although posts submitted to a group will always need to go through moderation). You can learn more about the rank ladder in the What's New post Let the Gamification Begin! If your post needs to go through moderation, please note that the moderation team may require up to five business days to conduct each review.

Recommendation: According to my own experience, the moderators usually publish your post sent for review directly if there are no further changes required. So ensure your content is really ready for publication before you send it for review.

Manage Your Content

You can find all your content in the "My Content" section.

Have You Noticed?

Table of Contents Button is Available

Allows you to create a table of contents, which provides audience with an overview of the blog content at the beginning of the blog.

Separate Buttons for Video Upload

Web video

Payment Inquiry Handling Agent - Demo video.mp4

SAP Media Share Video

 

Summary

In summary, this blog provides an overview of the key points of writing a blog post in the tool, and highlights the new features provided by the tool.
